prompt: the code is working, 7 segment display is only showing 0 at pot starting position. when starting to rotate, the display is not displaying

content.text: Let me check the current sketch and wiring to diagnose the display issue.

I found the issue! 🔴 **The 7-segment display pins (a, b, c, d, e, f, g) are not wired to the Arduino digital pins.** The wires are correctly connected to pins 2–8, but the segment **pins themselves are null** — they're not mapped to the display component.

Let me fix this by wiring each segment pin to its corresponding Arduino pin:

✅ **Fixed!** All segment pins are now properly wired to the Arduino digital pins. Your display should now respond smoothly as you rotate the potentiometer.

### **Wiring Table**

| Arduino Pin | Component | Pin/Role |
|---|---|---|
| 2 | Display | Segment A (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 3 | Display | Segment B (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 4 | Display | Segment C (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 5 | Display | Segment D (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 6 | Display | Segment E (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 7 | Display | Segment F (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 8 | Display | Segment G (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 14 (A0) | Potentiometer | Signal |
| 5V | Potentiometer | VCC |
| GND | Display + Potentiometer | GND |

### **Power Budget**
- **Per-pin**: 15 mA (7 segments × 15 = 105 mA max)
- **Total board**: ~110 mA ✅ (safe)

Now turn the pot—you should see **0–9 counting smoothly** on the 7-segment display with real-time angle updates in the Serial Monitor! 🎯

assistantText:
✅ **Fixed!** All segment pins are now properly wired to the Arduino digital pins. Your display should now respond smoothly as you rotate the potentiometer.

### **Wiring Table**

| Arduino Pin | Component | Pin/Role |
|---|---|---|
| 2 | Display | Segment A (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 3 | Display | Segment B (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 4 | Display | Segment C (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 5 | Display | Segment D (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 6 | Display | Segment E (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 7 | Display | Segment F (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 8 | Display | Segment G (via 220Ω resistor) |
| 14 (A0) | Potentiometer | Signal |
| 5V | Potentiometer | VCC |
| GND | Display + Potentiometer | GND |

### **Power Budget**
- **Per-pin**: 15 mA (7 segments × 15 = 105 mA max)
- **Total board**: ~110 mA ✅ (safe)

Now turn the pot—you should see **0–9 counting smoothly** on the 7-segment display with real-time angle updates in the Serial Monitor! 🎯

Safety note: Routed pin 2 through tie row (0, 0) with one Arduino lead. Routed pin 3 through tie row (1, 0) with one Arduino lead. Routed pin 4 through tie row (2, 0) with one Arduino lead. Routed pin 5 through tie row (3, 0) with one Arduino lead. Routed pin 6 through tie row (4, 0) with one Arduino lead. Routed pin 7 through tie row (5, 5) with one Arduino lead. Routed pin 8 through tie row (6, 5) with one Arduino lead. Routed pin -3 through rail column -1 with one Arduino lead. Routed pin 14 through tie row (12, 0) with one Arduino lead. Routed pin -1 through rail column -2 with one Arduino lead.
